name: verification-before-completion
description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
capabilities: Prevents claims of completion without running verification commands
Requires actual test output showing 0 failures before claiming tests pass
Blocks expressions of satisfaction like 'Great!' or 'Done!' before verification
Verifies agent delegation results independently before trusting reports
Enforces red-green cycles for regression test verification
author: obra
securitySummary: This skill contains ONLY behavioral instruction documentation (SKILL.md) with no executable code. All 48 static findings are false positives: markdown code block delimiters were misidentified as Ruby/shell backticks, YAML frontmatter field names were flagged as cryptographic content, and verification guidelines were misclassified as reconnaissance patterns. No external commands, network operations, filesystem access, or code execution capabilities exist. This is purely documentation that instructs AI agents to run verification commands themselves.
valueStatement: AI agents often claim work is complete without actual verification. This skill enforces evidence-based completion claims by requiring verification command output before any success assertions. It prevents false completion reports and builds trust through proof.
limitations: Requires the agent to have access to verification commands and their output
Does not execute verification itself - instructs the agent to run commands
Cannot prevent users from ignoring verification results

useCases.targetUser: Development teams using AI coding assistants
useCases.title: Prevent false completion reports
useCases.description: Stop AI agents from claiming tests pass or bugs fixed without running the actual verification commands.

useCases.title: Build trust in AI contributions
useCases.description: Ensure AI agents provide verification evidence before PRs, reducing rework from false completion claims.

useCases.title: Enforce verification discipline
useCases.description: Apply consistent verification standards across all AI agent work before accepting completion.

promptTemplates.title: Basic verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Before claiming this work is complete: 1) What command proves completion? 2) Run it now. 3) Show the output. 4) Only then state the status with evidence.

promptTemplates.title: Test verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Do not claim tests pass. Run the test command now. Report the actual output including failure counts. Show exit code if available.

promptTemplates.title: Build verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the build command and show the full output. Do not rely on linter passing or logs looking good. Report actual build status.

promptTemplates.title: Agent delegation
promptTemplates.prompt: The agent reports success. Before trusting this: Check the VCS diff for actual changes. Run relevant verification commands. Report what you actually verified.

bestPractices: Always run verification commands fresh - never trust previous runs or cached results
Report actual output including failure counts and exit codes, not interpretations
Apply verification to agent delegation - check VCS diffs and run independent verification
antiPatterns: Using words like 'should', 'probably', or 'seems to' instead of verified facts
Trusting agent success reports without independent verification of actual results

faq.question: Does this skill execute verification commands?
faq.answer: No. This skill instructs the agent to run verification commands and requires evidence before claims. The agent must execute commands.

faq.question: Can I skip verification for simple tasks?
faq.answer: No. The rule applies to all completion claims without exception, regardless of task complexity.

faq.question: What if verification commands are unavailable?
faq.answer: State that verification could not be performed and do not claim completion. Report the actual status with this limitation.

faq.question: Does this work with agent delegation?
faq.answer: Yes. When a sub-agent reports success, verify independently by checking VCS diffs and running verification commands.

faq.question: How does this handle partial verification?
faq.answer: Partial verification proves nothing. Full verification of the specific claim is required before any completion assertion.

faq.question: What about re-running recent verification?
faq.answer: Run verification fresh each time. Previous results, even from minutes ago, do not satisfy the verification requirement.
